sections.paragraph:
pr.n.-

(1) Edom, the son of Isaac, Jacob’s elder twin brother, Gen 25:25, more often called Esau (עֵשָׂו).

(2) the descendants of Edom, i.e. the nation of the Edomites or Idumæans; and also the country [Idumæa]. The nation is called more fully בְּנֵי אֱדֹם Psa 137:7 and poet. בַּת אֱדֹם “daughter of Edom,” La. 4:21 , 22 the country is more fully called אֶרֶץ אֱדֹם Gen 36:16 21:31 אֱדֹם Amo 1:6, and שְׂדֵה אֱדֹם Gen 32:4 Jud. 5:4 . When it stands alone it is of the masculine gender, where it denotes the people, Num 20:20 of the feminine when it means the land, Jer 49:17. The country of the Edomites consisted of the mountainous tract between the Dead Sea and the Ælanitic gulf of the Red Sea, afterwards called Gebalene, Γεβαληνή, now جبال Jebâl.

The gentile noun is אֲדֹמִי an Edomite, an Idumæan, Deu 23:8 fem. אֲדֹמִית, pl. אֲדֹמִיֹּות Idumæan (women), 1Ki 11:1.
